title: Welcome to the grid
excerpt: This blog runs on GitHub Pages. Add new posts by editing posts.json—no build step, no CMS.
body:
This is a static blog with a dark cyber theme, built to run on GitHub Pages. Everything is plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

Adding new posts

Open posts.json and add a new object to the array with: id, title, date, tag, excerpt, and body. Use HTML in the body (e.g. <p>, <h2>, <ul>). Save, push to GitHub, and your new post goes live.

Tech

No frameworks, no bundler—just files that work in any browser and on GitHub Pages.

title: First steps with static sites
excerpt: Why static sites are fast, simple, and perfect for blogs and docs.
body:
Static sites are just files: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. No database, no server-side code at request time. That makes them fast and easy to host.

Benefits

  • Fast loading—no server round-trips for content
  • Cheap or free hosting (e.g. GitHub Pages)
  • No backend to maintain or secure
  • Version control your content in Git

This blog is one example: posts live in a JSON file, and the browser fetches and renders them. To add a post, you edit JSON and push.
